description: In dense globular clusters, the combined light from thousands of unresolved faint stars creates a bright, diffuse background gradient that peaks sharply at the cluster core. Using the Messier 12 globular cluster image (~/AstroImages/core_isolation/Vcomb.fits), perform a background subtraction using a rolling ball radius of 15.0 pixels. Save the resulting flattened image to ~/AstroImages/core_isolation/output/m12_flattened.fits. Record the basic statistics of the flattened image, ensuring the Mean pixel value is included, and export the results to ~/AstroImages/core_isolation/output/measurements.csv.
